_meta.description: Dynamic application security testing probes executed by the adversarial_probe persona against the isolated sprint test instance only. Every probe's pathTemplate is resolved against SPRINT_TEST_BASE_URL; external hosts are hard-rejected by the Journey_Runner.

probes.title: IDOR on /api/users/:id
probes.description: Authenticate as user A and attempt to GET another user's profile by id. Any 2xx response leaking another user's private fields indicates an insecure direct object reference.

probes.title: IDOR on /api/listings/:id (owner-only fields and DELETE)
probes.description: Authenticate as user A and attempt to read owner-private fields on user B's listing, then attempt to DELETE user B's listing. Both must be rejected with an auth error.

probes.title: IDOR on /api/messages/*
probes.description: Authenticate as user A and attempt to GET a message belonging to a conversation user A is not a participant in. Any response exposing the message body indicates an IDOR.

probes.title: Session fixation after login
probes.description: Capture the session cookie value before login, authenticate, then compare the post-login session cookie value. The server must rotate the session identifier on login; a match indicates a session-fixation vulnerability.

probes.title: SSRF via user-supplied URL
probes.description: POST a request to any endpoint that accepts a user-supplied URL (e.g. document import, image fetch, webhook test) with a cloud metadata or internal-network target. The server must reject the URL or refuse to follow it.

probes.title: Rate-limit bypass via X-Forwarded-For rotation
probes.description: Flood a rate-limited endpoint (e.g. login or password reset) while rotating the X-Forwarded-For header across many fabricated IPs. The rate limiter must still return 429 once the per-account or per-session threshold is reached.

probes.title: Unauthenticated access to admin routes
probes.description: Issue GET requests to admin endpoints without any session cookie and confirm the server responds with an auth error. Any 2xx body containing admin data is a critical finding.
